data.title: Stocks dip as Trump hints at Iran strike, oil hits 7-month high at $66.65/barrel. S&P 500 down 0.3%, Nasdaq and Dow down 0.5%. Energy sector gains 0.8%, Occidental Petroleum (NYSA: OXY) up 9% and Deere & Company (NYSA: DE) up 12.6%.
data.description: U.S. stocks dipped as President Trump hinted at a possible military intervention in Iran, pushing crude prices up for a second day. West Texas Intermediate futures surged 2.5% to $66.65 per barrel, the highest since August 2025. The S&P 500 fell by 0.3%, Nasdaq 100 and Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped about 0.5%. Energy stocks rose 0.8% while financials lagged. Occidental Petroleum Corp. shares surged 9%, while Deere & Company jumped 12.6%, leading to a 27% month-to-date gain.

data.title: Fujifilm to showcase advanced lithography research at SPIE Conference, aiming to double semiconductor revenue to $3.26B by 2030.
data.description: In Tokyo, FUJIFILM Corporation is set to showcase its recent research discoveries at SPIE Advanced Lithography + Patterning 2026 from February 22-26, 2026, in San Jose, California. The event, managed by SPIE, the worldwide group for optics and photonics, serves as a platform for unveiling the company's vision. As FUJIFILM pursues a growth strategy to augment revenue in the semiconductor materials sector, aiming to reach 500 billion yen (equivalent to 3.26 billion USD) by FY2030 in contrast to FY2024, the focus lies on this key objective to enhance its market presence.

data.title: The Pokémon Company International buys Excell Brands, a top U.S. distributor and service provider.
data.description: Bellevue, Washington - The Pokémon Company International has announced its acquisition of Excell Brands, a prominent U.S. distributor and provider of category management services. This move signifies a strategic development in the company's expansion efforts, involving an undisclosed financial transaction as part of the acquisition deal. This acquisition is expected to enhance The Pokémon Company International's market presence and streamline its distribution operations in the U.S.

data.title: Velocys achieves over 30% cost reduction in microFTL technology deployment, advancing 2 flagship FT SAF projects.
data.description: Houston and Oxford, England - Velocys revealed on February 19, 2026, that they have successfully enhanced manufacturing and delivery processes resulting in a significant reduction of over 30% in the total investment cost for their microFTL™ technology. These improvements were validated during the design phase, showcasing the company's commitment to cost-effectiveness.

data.title: Broadcom launches game-changing BroadPeak radio digital front-end SoC for 5G/6G, offering up to 40% power reduction and advanced tech.
data.description: Broadcom has launched the BroadPeak radio digital front-end SoC device aimed at enhancing 5G and 6G wireless infrastructure. This is the first digital radio front-end to meet upcoming 5G Advanced requirements in the n104 band and future 6G upper mid-band needs. The device promises up to a 40% power reduction compared to existing solutions and features state-of-the-art 5nm CMOS technology. Broadcom has started shipping samples of the BCM85021 to early access customers. This news caused Broadcom's shares to rise, contrasting the broader market's decline with major indices like S&P 500 and Nasdaq closing lower. The device positions Broadcom as a key player in the upcoming 5G Advanced and 6G standards, catering to the increasing demand for enhanced mobile network capabilities.

data.title: Clearmind Medicine is researching MEAI and GLP-1 for weight loss, aiming to revolutionize the obesity treatment market.
data.description: In Hong Kong on February 19, 2026, Clearmind Medicine, a clinical-stage biotech company listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol CMND, is making strides in the obesity treatment market which is valued at billions of dollars. They are exploring innovative therapeutic methods beyond GLP-1–based treatments.

data.title: AB Volvo's Election Committee proposes re-electing Chairman Pär Boman and Board members; key names in lineup.
data.description: The Election Committee of AB Volvo in Gothenburg, Sweden, announced on Feb. 19, 2026, their proposal for the re-election of Chairman Pär Boman and several Board members including Bo Annvik, Jan Carlson, Eric Elzvik, and Martha Finn. This decision comes as part of their efforts to ensure the company's governance remains stable and competent. The proposal reflects the Committee's confidence in the current leadership and aims to maintain continuity within the Board. This move signifies the importance of sustained leadership for AB Volvo's future direction and decision-making processes.

data.title: Immigrant truck drivers in California demand action as thousands of CDLs set to expire on March 6, with immigrants making up 1 in 5 truckers.
data.description: In Fresno, California on February 19, 2026, the Freedom Drivers group, made up of immigrant truck drivers, is urging swift action as numerous commercial driver's licenses (CDLs) are due to expire on March 6. Immigrants make up almost 20% of truck drivers across the country.

data.title: InventHelp inventor creates new fidget toy OODLES GRIP (RKH-956), aims to reduce restlessness and anxiety.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on February 19, 2026, an inventor from Detroit, Michigan introduced the OODLES GRIP, a fidget toy designed to alleviate nervous energy and calm restlessness and anxiety. The inventor aimed to address situations like waiting for appointments. The toy aims to offer a tactile solution to help individuals manage their anxiety and fidgeting habits. This innovation showcases the entrepreneur's dedication to providing a tool that can potentially benefit individuals dealing with varying levels of anxiety and restlessness.

data.title: VHHA Solutions partners with Andor Health® to enhance care delivery with ThinkAndor® AI platform for hospitals and health systems.
data.description: A recent collaboration aims to provide technology solutions for enhancing care delivery in various healthcare settings such as hospitals, health systems, and non-acute facilities in the community. This partnership will also assist in aligning with key CMS programs like the ACCESS Model and Rural Health Transformation. The initiative aims to improve healthcare services by leveraging technology tools to foster efficient and effective care provision across organizations.

data.title: Fleetworthy consolidates brands to enhance fleet readiness, aiming for cost control. Feb. 19, 2026.
data.description: Albany, New York - On February 19, 2026, Fleetworthy, a leading technology suite for fleet readiness, revealed the consolidation of its brands into one unified Fleetworthy brand. This move signifies a significant advancement towards a more integrated fleet readiness process. This change comes as the company aims to streamline its offerings and enhance the overall customer experience. The move unites all its services under one brand, including Bestpass.

data.title: Hedge funds reveal Q4 portfolios, major investors make bold moves: SoftBank sells $6B Nvidia, Buffett cuts 77% Amazon stake, Druckenmiller dumps 30+ positions. Notable names like Warren Buffett, Bill Ackman update portfolios. Biggest moves: SoftBank into crypto, Buffett's $352M New York Times stake, Ackman's $1.76B in Meta; Druckenmiller focuses on financials, Brazil, equal-weight S&P. AI developments: Meta signs Nvidia chip deal, boosting both stocks. Zuckerberg touts "personal superintelligence." Full details on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Hedge funds recently disclosed their Q4 portfolios, revealing significant shifts in investments. SoftBank sold its entire $6B Nvidia position and moved into crypto, including $781M in a Bitcoin treasury company. Warren Buffett reduced his Amazon stake by 77% and acquired a $352M New York Times position. Investment giants like Bill Ackman and Druckenmiller also made notable changes to their portfolios. Druckenmiller liquidated over 30 holdings and increased exposure to financials, Brazil, and equal-weight S&P. Meta signed a deal to utilize millions of Nvidia chips in its data centers, boosting Nvidia's stock by ~2%. The semiconductor index also saw a 1.4% rise. Mark Zuckerberg highlighted this move as a step towards "personal superintelligence for everyone."

data.title: NVIDIA MIG technology boosts AI workload speed by up to 2.25x under power constraints, reducing infrastructure costs.
data.description: NVIDIA's latest Multi-Instance GPU technology demonstrates a significant increase in performance, showcasing up to a 2.25 times speed improvement for data center workloads while operating within set power limits. This advancement has notable implications for reducing AI infrastructure expenses.

data.title: Mattel reveals expansive Masters of the Universe publishing program ahead of film release, launching in 2026.
data.description: Mattel, Inc. (Nasdaq: MAT), headquartered in El Segundo, California, unveiled an ambitious publishing initiative for its renowned Masters of the Universe brand aimed at rolling out throughout 2026. The initiative will be carried out through Mattel Press, the company’s proprietary publishing arm, alongside collaborators. This move is part of Mattel’s overarching strategy to broaden the reach of the franchise to fresh and varied demographics globally through various mediums such as toys, films, digital platforms, and consumer products.

data.title: DPR Construction opens new Silicon Valley office and prefab facility for safer, more efficient project delivery on Feb. 19, 2026.
data.description: A recently launched campus in Santa Clara, California has combined office and production capabilities to enhance project delivery for clients in the Bay Area and across the West Coast. DPR Construction celebrated the inauguration of its Silicon Valley Office on February 19, 2026. This integrated facility aims to improve efficiency and safety for customers.
